title: Post-Quantum’s Classic McEliece Achieves Global ISO Standardization - Quantum Computing Report
gist: UK cybersecurity firm Post-Quantum's Classic McEliece algorithm became the first post-quantum cryptography algorithm to achieve ISO standardization (ISO/IEC 18033-2), giving it a regulatory edge over lattice-based alternatives like CRYSTALS-Kyber for defending against 'harvest now, decrypt later' attacks.

insights.claim: Classic McEliece, developed by UK firm Post-Quantum, is the first post-quantum cryptography algorithm to achieve global ISO standardization under ISO/IEC 18033-2, covering 177 ISO member states.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Being first to ISO standardization gives Post-Quantum a regulatory and procurement edge with government/defense buyers who require internationally validated benchmarks before adopting PQC infrastructure.
insights.quote: the code-based cryptographic protocol is the first post-quantum cryptography (PQC) algorithm to secure global standardization under this specification

insights.claim: Classic McEliece is based on a 1978 code-based cryptosystem (Robert McEliece) that has resisted cryptanalysis for nearly five decades, differentiating it from lattice-based PQC alternatives such as CRYSTALS-Kyber on security track record, though it requires much larger public keys (255KB-1.3MB) in exchange for the smallest ciphertext payload (under 208 bytes) among known PQC KEMs.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: The key-size/ciphertext-size tradeoff versus lattice-based schemes shapes which PQC algorithm wins in bandwidth-constrained vs. storage-constrained deployments — relevant for assessing which vendors/algorithms dominate specific infrastructure segments.
insights.quote: it produces the smallest ciphertext payload (under 208 bytes) among all known post-quantum Key Encapsulation Mechanisms (KEMs), and its public keys are entirely reusable

insights.claim: Germany's BSI and the Dutch National Cyber Security Centre already recommend Classic McEliece specifically for protecting long-shelf-life data like healthcare records and intellectual property.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: National cybersecurity agency endorsements are a leading indicator of procurement momentum in the EU public sector for Post-Quantum's technology.
insights.quote: Classic McEliece is already recommended by Germany’s BSI and the Dutch National Cyber Security Centre to protect long-shelf-life data

insights.claim: Post-Quantum partnered with Czech defense manufacturer STV Group to deploy Classic McEliece on battlefield-ready drones operating in communication-denied environments, demonstrating the algorithm works on constrained edge hardware despite its large key sizes.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Successful edge/defense deployment counters the common objection that large PQC key sizes are impractical for constrained hardware, opening a defense-tech commercialization path for Post-Quantum.
insights.quote: Post-Quantum recently dispelled historical assumptions regarding key-size limits on edge hardware by partnering with Czech defense manufacturer STV Group to deploy the algorithm on battlefield-ready drones
